subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Interpret the poem 'A Concrete Example' and its central idea
Analyse the character of Mrs. Jones and her unique gardening style
Identify literary devices such as imagery, humour, irony, and alliteration
Understand how wordplay and symbolism contribute to the meaning of the poem
Develop reading comprehension, vocabulary, and analytical skills

lesson_plan.title: Introducing the Poem and Its Context
topics_covered: Introduction to the poem and its theme
student_activities: Discuss the meaning of 'concrete' in different contexts
Share ideas about what makes a garden beautiful

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehending the Poem
topics_covered: Understanding the description of Mrs. Jones’s garden
Identifying key images in the poem
student_activities: Highlight descriptive phrases that create images

lesson_plan.title: Character and Imagery Analysis
topics_covered: Imagery used to describe the garden
student_activities: Draw or describe Mrs. Jones’s garden
Identify lines that reflect her personality

lesson_plan.title: Literary Devices and Tone
student_activities: Discuss how the ending creates humour

lesson_plan.title: Theme and Language Practice
topics_covered: Theme of different perspectives on beauty
student_activities: Write a short paragraph about the poem’s message
Complete vocabulary and word formation exercises

teaching_aids: Pictures of different types of gardens
teacher_tip: Encourage students to compare their own idea of a beautiful garden with Mrs. Jones’s unusual garden. This helps them understand the poem’s theme about different perspectives.
